record_meta.disclaimer: All information in this chart is synthetic and generated for testing/demo use.

medications.name: Metformin ER 1000 mg
medications.sig: Take 1 tablet by mouth twice daily with meals

medications.name: Omeprazole 20 mg
medications.sig: Take 1 capsule by mouth daily before breakfast as needed

medications.name: Albuterol HFA 90 mcg
medications.sig: 2 puffs inhaled every 4-6 hours as needed for wheeze

encounters.vitals: BP 142/88, HR 78, Temp 98.4 F, RR 14, SpO2 98%, Wt 184 lb
encounters.reason: Annual preventive visit and chronic disease follow-up.
assessment: Type 2 diabetes: A1c above goal at 7.8%. Discussed diet consistency and exercise plan.
Hypertension: BP persistently above goal; changed from lisinopril (cough) to losartan.
Hyperlipidemia: Continue statin; repeat fasting lipids in 6 months.
plan: Increase metformin ER to 1000 mg twice daily.
Order CMP, A1c, urine microalbumin, fasting lipid panel.
Vaccinate with PCV20 due to diabetes risk profile.

encounters.reason: 3-day cough, wheezing, sinus congestion after viral illness exposure.
assessment: Likely viral bronchitis with asthma exacerbation (mild).
No focal consolidation on exam; no severe respiratory distress.
plan: Supportive care and return precautions discussed.

encounters.reason: Intermittent substernal chest pressure x6 hours with mild nausea; improved on arrival.
assessment: Low-risk chest pain; serial troponins negative.
ECG: sinus tachycardia, no ST-elevation; nonspecific T-wave changes.
Symptoms possibly reflux-related; advised close PCP follow-up.
plan: Trial GI cocktail with symptom improvement.
Discharge with return precautions and PCP/cardiology follow-up if recurrence.

encounters.vitals: BP 132/82, HR 76, Temp 98.6 F, RR 14, SpO2 98%, Wt 182 lb
encounters.reason: Post-ED follow-up for chest pain and chronic disease review.
assessment: No recurrent chest pain since ED discharge; likely GERD contributor.
BP improved with losartan; home readings averaging 128-134/78-84.
A1c trending down; patient increased walking to 4 days/week.
plan: Continue current meds; use omeprazole daily for 4 weeks then PRN.
Refer for outpatient exercise stress test due to risk factors and ED visit.

encounters.vitals: BP 128/80, HR 74, Temp 98.1 F, RR 14, SpO2 99%, Wt 181 lb
assessment: A1c improved to 7.1%; still above individualized target <7.0%.
No nephropathy or retinopathy documented; annual eye exam due next month.
Discussed GLP-1 RA option for glycemic and weight benefit.
plan: Start semaglutide 0.25 mg weekly x4 weeks, then 0.5 mg weekly if tolerated.
Monitor fasting glucose 3-4 times/week and maintain food/activity log.

ecg.summary: Sinus tachycardia (rate 103 bpm), normal axis, QTc 437 ms, nonspecific T-wave flattening in lateral leads; no STEMI criteria.

cxr.summary: Single-view chest radiograph: cardiac silhouette within normal size limits, no focal airspace opacity, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax. No acute osseous abnormality on this exam.

stress_test.summary: Exercise treadmill test completed 9:21 Bruce protocol; no ischemic ST changes, no arrhythmia, appropriate BP response. Low-risk study.

ultrasound.summary: RUQ ultrasound for episodic abdominal discomfort: mild hepatic steatosis, no cholelithiasis, no biliary dilation.

findings: Chronically inflamed gallbladder with multiple cholesterol stones.
Critical view of safety obtained prior to clipping cystic duct/artery.
No bile leak noted after irrigation and hemostasis check.
surgical_report.specimen_sent: Gallbladder submitted to pathology in formalin.
surgical_report.disposition: Transferred to PACU in stable condition.

pathology_report.gross_description: 7.8 x 3.1 x 2.9 cm gallbladder with green-brown bile and multiple yellow stones up to 0.8 cm.
pathology_report.microscopic_description: Mucosal flattening with chronic inflammatory infiltrate and focal Rokitansky-Aschoff sinus formation.

discharge_summary.admission_diagnosis: Chest pain, rule out acute coronary syndrome
discharge_summary.discharge_diagnosis: Low-risk chest pain, likely reflux-related; hypertension; type 2 diabetes mellitus
hospital_course: Presented with intermittent substernal pressure with mild nausea.
Serial high-sensitivity troponins remained negative and ECG showed no acute ischemic changes.
Symptoms improved with GI cocktail and observation; remained hemodynamically stable overnight.
discharge_medications: Metformin ER 1000 mg PO twice daily.
Omeprazole 20 mg PO daily for 4 weeks then PRN.
follow_up: Cardiology follow-up within 2-4 weeks if recurrent symptoms.
Return to ED immediately for persistent chest pain, dyspnea, syncope, or new neurologic symptoms.

consult_note.reason: Evaluation after ED chest pain episode and multiple cardiovascular risk factors.
consult_note.history: No recurrent exertional chest pain. Walks 30 minutes 4 times weekly. Denies syncope, orthopnea, edema, or palpitations.
assessment: Atypical chest pain with reassuring ED and outpatient treadmill workup.
ASCVD risk elevated due to diabetes, hypertension, and dyslipidemia.
Blood pressure and lipid control improved on current regimen.
recommendations: Continue losartan and rosuvastatin without change.
Maintain structured exercise and Mediterranean-style diet.
No additional ischemic testing unless symptom pattern changes.
Follow up in cardiology clinic in 6 months.

anesthesia_report.airway: Mallampati II, Grade I view with MAC 3 blade
events: Intubation successful on first attempt with 7.0 cuffed ETT.
Hemodynamics stable throughout case; no vasopressor requirement.
Extubated awake in OR and transferred to PACU on supplemental oxygen.
anesthesia_report.post_anesthesia: Pain controlled, no post-op nausea/vomiting, Aldrete 9 on arrival to PACU.
